title: Positive clause (P1): every automorphism is Sigma^in_1-definable from finitely many parameters

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Sub-lemma (G3 / P1).** Show $A$ is computably $\AUT$-countable on a cone.

By Theorem 2.3 (after Ash–Knight–Manasse–Slaman 1989) this is equivalent to: for each $\pi\in\AUT(A)$ there exists *some* finite tuple $\bar a$ (depending on $\pi$) such that $\pi$ is $\Sigma^{in}_1$-definable from $\bar a$, i.e. there is a family of existential formulas $(\phi_i)_{i\in\N}$ with $\pi(x)=y\iff\exists i\,A\models\phi_i(\bar a,x,y)$.

Proof obligation: assuming each $\pi$ has finite support (G2), name one base point inside each moved gadget and exhibit the explicit family $(\phi_i)$ defining the within-gadget swap. 

Caveats to handle carefully:
- Do **not** assume a single existential formula suffices — the definition allows a *family* $(\phi_i)$.
- Handle the 'over a cone' relativization correctly (AKMS gives c.e.-on-a-cone $\Leftrightarrow$ $\Sigma^{in}_1$-with-parameters; verify the hypotheses of that theorem in this setting).
